title: The Best New Bars in Miami

whatHappening: Miami's bar scene is experiencing a renaissance with new establishments showcasing vermouth and sherry-based cocktails alongside classic Spanish drinks. The city's mixology landscape is evolving beyond its traditional rum and agave spirit foundations to embrace Spanish and European cocktail culture.
whoInvolved: Eater Miami is documenting this shift in the hospitality scene, highlighting bartenders and bar owners who are introducing these new drink categories to the local market. The venues represent a broader movement among Miami's hospitality professionals to diversify their cocktail offerings.
whyMatters: This trend reflects Miami's unique position as a cultural crossroads and signals how consumer preferences are shifting toward exploration of lesser-known spirits and traditional European cocktails. moreContext: Miami has historically leveraged its geographic position between the U.S., Caribbean, and Latin America to champion diverse culinary and beverage traditions, with rum and agave spirits becoming staples in local bars. This shift mirrors similar movements in other major drinking cities where craft cocktail culture has matured and consumers seek authenticity and historical accuracy in their drinks. The expansion of these categories also reflects increased interest in aperitif culture and low-ABV cocktails among health-conscious consumers.

title: Where Elegance Lives: 5 Wonderful Miami Bars
summary: One of my go-to spots when I’m in New York City is the iconic King Cole Bar at the St. Regis. Stepping inside always feels like an immersion into a bygone era—a time when elegance was a deeply desired way of life. Even as modern fashion has shifted toward the casual, the bar…
whatHappening: Miami Curated is highlighting five elegant bars in Miami that embody classic sophistication and timeless design. The piece draws inspiration from iconic establishments like the King Cole Bar at the St. Regis in New York, celebrating venues where elegance remains a defining characteristic.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated, a local lifestyle publication, is curating this list of upscale bar destinations. The King Cole Bar at the St. Regis represents the gold standard of sophisticated hospitality that these Miami establishments aspire to match.
whyMatters: As casual dining and dress codes continue to relax across the industry, these bars preserve spaces where traditional elegance and refined experiences still matter to discerning patrons. This cultural preservation helps maintain Miami's reputation as a destination for luxury experiences and high-end hospitality.
moreContext: The King Cole Bar, established in 1904, is one of America's most storied cocktail lounges and serves as a benchmark for old-world elegance in hospitality. Despite fashion and social norms shifting toward casualness in recent decades, a subset of travelers and locals actively seek out venues that maintain dress codes and formal atmospheres. Miami has positioned itself as a luxury destination competing with established cities like New York and New Orleans for high-end hospitality clientele. These curated bar lists serve both as destination guides and as cultural commentary on the enduring appeal of sophisticated, vintage-inspired spaces.

title: From Casual Lunch to Serious Dinner: Paperfish Sushi Brickell Stepped Up Its Game

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour in Miami Beach is establishing itself as a premier lifestyle destination that prioritizes wellness and local culture over tourist chaos. The neighborhood is drawing residents and visitors who seek an alternative to the frenetic energy of Ocean Drive.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is covering this emerging neighborhood trend. Sunset Harbour itself is a Miami Beach community located on the bay side of South Beach, increasingly populated by wellness-focused businesses and local establishments.
whyMatters: This shift reflects broader Miami trends toward curated, neighborhood-focused experiences rather than mass tourism. The success of Sunset Harbour could influence how other Miami Beach areas market themselves and attract a more affluent, lifestyle-conscious demographic.
moreContext: Sunset Harbour sits on the quieter, bay-facing side of South Beach, positioning it as an alternative to the party-centric Ocean Drive corridor. The neighborhood has evolved to emphasize walkability, local dining, wellness amenities, and residential appeal. This trend aligns with post-pandemic preferences for less crowded urban spaces and health-conscious living. Miami Beach's neighborhood stratification—from tourist zones to lifestyle destinations—is reshaping the city's identity and real estate values.
